attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 EMD (Endocrine & Metabolic Disorders) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. This is NET-NEW coverage (the 9th tranche-1 category, not a base-303 refresh) — ingest grows the eval corpus 403->453. This is a BIDIRECTIONAL endocrine/metabolic domain, and the folder is authored so that every condition presents via an ACUTE COMPLICATION / DECOMPENSATION — tiers are matched to the acute complication described, not the chronic label. Tier calibration reviewed and accepted: baseline T2x1 / T3x12 / T4x13 / T5x24. This high-acuity weighting is honest for an all-acute-complication folder, confirmed by reading source notes (batch-1 sanity check + downward-discrimination spot-checks), NOT reflexive over-triage — forcing lower tiers would under-triage genuine metabolic emergencies (under-triage weighted 3x). ENDOCRINE/METABOLIC EMERGENCIES tier to true acuity with 000 escalation (T5): diabetic ketoacidosis, hyperosmolar hyperglycaemic state, myxoedema coma, adrenal/Addisonian crisis, thyroid storm, phaeochromocytoma crisis, severe electrolyte derangement (hyper- and hypocalcaemic crises / tetany), congenital-adrenal-hyperplasia neonatal salt-wasting, MEN/gastrinoma crisis. MASS-EFFECT / UNEXCLUDABLE-MIMIC presentations tier T3-T4 recognise + urgent referral/imaging (acromegaly/prolactinoma with optic-chiasm compression, thyroid cancer with airway compression, dyslipidaemia presenting as acute pancreatitis, primary hyperparathyroidism with renal colic, haemochromatosis with new atrial fibrillation, Cushing's with severe hypertension). DOWNWARD DISCRIMINATION CONFIRMED: pre-diabetes (EMD-038) correctly floored at T2 (genuinely mild source: candidal intertrigo, Risk Low, routine 3-5 day GP disposition) — the clause discriminates low as well as high, and the 12 T3s sit at the telehealth 'AI cannot examine / cannot obtain bloods -> urgent same-day in-person' floor. A T4/T5-ceiling guardrail was applied (an in-person exam or pending lab done 'to be sure' is not by itself a T4/T5 justification; reserve emergency tiers for patient-reportable time-critical features) and held throughout. Telehealth lab-dependence honoured: NO fabricated lab / hormone / electrolyte values; a patient-obtained bedside glucometer / ketone-meter reading ships as the patient-stated STRING verbatim in objective_data_offered (verified:false), never parsed to a number and never category lab_result. Safety invariants honoured throughout: NO insulin, thyroxine, hydrocortisone, antihypertensive, or oral-hypoglycaemic dose minted as AI output (all recorded doses are the CLINICIAN answer sealed in node 12, should_NOT_recommend / not_indicated_here for the AI); NO paediatric / weight-based dose (hard limit, live in CAH and paediatric diabetes cases); no autonomous diagnosis; failure to recognise/escalate a metabolic emergency encoded as the prime error-of-omission. Specific borderlines reviewed and accepted: (a) EMD-038 pre-diabetes -> T2 (genuine mild presentation, not an under-triage); (b) EMD-036 PCOS -> T4 — the 'PCOS' label is a red herring; the scored acute entity is heavy dysfunctional uterine bleeding + severe pelvic pain with an UNEXCLUDABLE ectopic pregnancy / ovarian torsion / significant haemorrhage that the AI cannot exclude remotely (no bedside beta-hCG), so urgent escalation is correct; (c) EMD-011 empty sella -> T5 (source note itself activated 000 for a suspected spontaneous CSF leak with visual-field loss); (d) EMD-012 gestational diabetes -> T4 (third-trimester reduced fetal movements — a stillbirth-risk red flag the AI cannot assess by auscultation).
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all EMD casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Patient age not stated in source note; inferred older_adult (~70) from chronic thiazide + calcium-supplement use and the hypercalcaemia picture — maintainer to confirm on clinician review.
Provisional case_id SEQ 00016 (from source EMD-016) — ingest --reseq will assign the real sequence within the SPEC-ENDO-03 bucket.
Specialty assigned ENDO (Endocrine & Metabolic) with secondary EMG (emergency framing) — reviewer may re-prioritise; the primary problem is a metabolic/electrolyte emergency.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person out-of-hours home visit (clinician-measured BP 90/55, HR 115, SpO2 96%, examined power 2/5, absent bowel sounds/ileus, and arranged IV fluids + cardiac monitoring). Clinician-measured vitals, neuro exam and ileus are sealed in 10/11 as clinician-only; carer-reportable observations (drowsiness, weakness, vomiting, dry lips, empty water jugs, palpitations) are in 01.objective_data_offered. IV rehydration and calcium-lowering therapy are reprojected to recognise + escalate, not AI actions.

No dose synthesised: the definitive treatment (IV fluids, bisphosphonate/calcitonin) is hospital-delivered and deliberately dose-free; the thiazide and calcium supplement are marked should_NOT_recommend (withhold).

Diagnosis uncertainty preserved: certainty_achievable_via_telehealth = cannot_diagnose_remotely — the specific cation is unknowable without hospital bloods; hypernatraemia and potassium derangements are retained on the differential.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
